title: Chrysanthemum Tea

seo.title: Chrysanthemum Tea Recipe: Easy & Authentic Chinese Brew
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Chinese Chrysanthemum Tea at home with our easy recipe. Learn the benefits and enjoy a soothing, fragrant brew. Perfect for any time!
h2Headings: How to Make Chrysanthemum Tea: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Perfect Brew
seo.imageAlt: A steaming cup of authentic Chinese Chrysanthemum Tea with dried chrysanthemum flowers beside it, ready to be enjoyed.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a soothing cup of homemade Chrysanthemum Tea, a traditional Chinese beverage known for its delicate flavor and health benefits.

description: Chrysanthemum Tea is a fragrant and soothing herbal beverage deeply rooted in Chinese culture. Made from dried chrysanthemum flowers, it boasts a delicate floral aroma and a subtly sweet flavor. This calming brew is not only refreshing but also cherished for its traditional health benefits.

instructions.title: Prepare Chrysanthemum Flowers
instructions.text: Place the dried chrysanthemum flowers in a fine-mesh sieve and gently rinse them under cool running water. This helps remove any dust or impurities. Set aside to drain briefly.

instructions.title: Boil Water
instructions.text: Bring 4 cups of filtered water to a boil in a kettle or saucepan. Once boiling, allow it to cool for about 30 seconds to a minute, aiming for a temperature of around 90-95°C (195-205°F).

instructions.title: Add Flowers to Teapot
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the rinsed chrysanthemum flowers into a heatproof teapot or a large tea infuser basket. Ensure the flowers have enough space to expand as they steep.

instructions.title: First Steep (Rinse)
instructions.text: Pour a small amount of the hot water over the chrysanthemum flowers, just enough to cover them. Swirl gently for 5-10 seconds, then immediately discard this water. This quick rinse further cleanses the flowers and awakens their fragrance.

instructions.title: Main Steep
instructions.text: Pour the remaining hot water over the chrysanthemum flowers. Cover the teapot with a lid and let the tea steep for 3 to 5 minutes, or longer for a stronger flavor. Observe the flowers unfurl and the tea turn a beautiful golden yellow.

instructions.title: Sweeten and Serve
instructions.text: Strain the tea into individual cups, or remove the infuser. Add rock sugar or honey to your desired sweetness, if using. Serve hot and enjoy the delicate aroma and soothing taste of fresh chrysanthemum tea.

tips: For the best flavor, use good quality dried chrysanthemum flowers. Hangzhou 'Hang Ju' (杭菊) and Bo Ju (亳菊) are popular varieties known for their fragrance and taste.
Avoid over-steeping as it can make the tea bitter. Adjust steeping time based on your preference for strength, typically 3-5 minutes is ideal.
Chrysanthemum tea can be enjoyed hot or chilled. For iced tea, let it cool completely, then refrigerate. It can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days.
article: Chrysanthemum Tea is a beloved Chinese Beverages dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
